summary: vitest-pro typosquat with postinstall reverse-shell dropper to 74.0.48.37
details: Package impersonates the popular vitest test runner: name is `vitest-pro`, `main` is `lib/nodemailer.js` (a verbatim copy of nodemailer source used as cover), the `description` field is unrelated boilerplate ("Copyright (c) React Training 2015-present"), and the `author` is set to nodemailer's real maintainer while `homepage` points at an unrelated `vitest-pro.com`. `package.json` declares `"postinstall": "node lib/utils/index.js"`, which on every `npm install` spawns `lib/utils/smtp-connection/index.js` detached and unref'd so the install command appears to complete silently. That file is heavily obfuscated with a custom base91-style decoder (per-function alphabets `Kk8eRJ9`, `TUJEDC`, `rsRi5N`, `ROnpYY`, `owUZnv`, `OrU3aA`, etc.) operating over a shared rotated string array. The decoder reconstructs the hardcoded IPv4 `74.0.48.37` and ports `0x11cc`/`0x11ce` (4556/4558), then invokes `child_process.spawn` with an `-e` argument to execute attacker-supplied code, with an additional Linux-gated branch (`process.platform == 'linux'`). Net effect on the installer: arbitrary remote code execution on the host the moment `npm install vitest-pro` runs, with the connection back to a hardcoded numeric C2.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
